justification: The plan identifies `sendLoginEmail` discarding the canonical username, but contains no explicit "Causal chain" section, complete dedup result, or sibling-repo check for the shared auth mechanism.
The plan claims "Confidence: 5/5" from the observed boundary timing but provides no fault injection or focused reproduction that induces the blamed clock-boundary cause.
The supplied plan says "the 12 prior stabilization commits did that and the flake recurred each time," establishing a chronic family with at least two failed merged fixes.
It states two prior fixes "are already completed and this June 27 recurrence happened after those changes" and claims 5/5 without inducing the CloudFront-limit mechanism.
The plan claims "Confidence: 5/5" for the collision classifier without a focused reproduction that induces the identity collision and proves the fresh-identity retry.
Although the invariant is traced to the global `GoogleMapsSdkProvider`, the plan omits the required explicit "Causal chain" and sibling-repo check sections for an app-bootstrap mechanism.
The workflow evidence shows blank `EXPECTED_DEPLOYED_COMMIT_SHA`, but the plan has no explicit "Causal chain" section or sibling-repo check for the shared remote-E2E workflow mechanism.
It admits "The exact reason the dialog disappeared is inferred" yet assigns 4/5 confidence and retries the test-side click instead of terminating the product-side remount/disappearance chain.
The low-minute boundary and future-time validation are evidenced, but the plan lacks an explicit "Causal chain" section and sibling-repo check for the shared timekeeping harness mechanism.
The plan lists three unresolved causes—propagation, premature backend success, or throttling—while requiring a code fix at 4/5 confidence and omitting A7 validation with Playwright retries disabled.
The snapshot names a "prior implementation for the same family" plus prior test-only hardenings, while admitting "exact identification of the competing history writer" is still missing.
The stale `offsetX` completion check is tied to specific component lines, but the plan still omits the mandatory explicit "Causal chain" and sibling-repo check sections.

The snapshot says "Previous investigations ... canonical earlier occurrences" and directs comparison with a "previous fix," but supplies neither a Prior attempts section nor an implementation plan classifiable under A/C/D.
The plan says "The exact React state transition that collapsed/unmounted the visits subtree is inferred" but proposes repeated test clicks at 4/5 confidence.
The plan cites prior "consumer-side mitigations 152fe5ea8d7/32c31abeeef" but has no Prior attempts, confidence, current-main, causal-chain, dedup, or full sibling-repo sections.

The plan has not resolved whether writes and reads use different services/databases, yet proposes a test-side poll without a stated bound and says the final fix "may be either" harness hardening or service alignment.
It admits "the exact backend/auth reason why license-manager returned 401" is unknown, so the causal chain terminates at a status code while a 4/5 code fix retries that status.
The current branch contains two "prior stabilizations" before this recurrence, while the plan admits the precise close/remount trigger is inferred and the harness fix "may mask a product bug."
The plan claims "Confidence: 5/5" from the observed late success but provides no fault injection or focused reproduction inducing the delayed-auth retry race.
Two prior fixes already classified and scoped the same worker-identity-collision retry mechanism, and this recurrence re-proposes extending that mechanism to the generic conflict response.
